rent.note: furnished 1BR in Palermo/Recoleta; USD blue-market pricing; range $400–$800

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; $50–$100 range at blue-rate USD equivalent

groceries.note: home cooking; supermarkets well-stocked; pricing in USD favorable at blue rate

diningOut.note: excellent restaurant scene; USD holders get ~2x purchasing power vs peso official rate

transit.note: subte + colectivos; very cheap at USD blue rate

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; peso pricing at blue rate = strong value for USD holders

simData.note: Claro/Personal/Movistar 30-day plan; buy in pesos at blue rate for best value

misc.note: gym, toiletries, entertainment, tango lessons, dining culture; buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.claim: solo nomad $900–$1,800/month depending on lifestyle; $1,412 comfortable average
evidence.quote: Monthly costs in Buenos Aires for a solo nomad currently run between $900 and $1,500, depending on lifestyle and neighborhood; most digital nomads can expect to spend $900 to $1,800 per month

evidence.claim: 1BR in Palermo/Recoleta $400–$800 USD at blue-market equivalent
evidence.quote: Expect $400-800 USD/month for a one-bedroom in popular neighborhoods like Palermo or Recoleta

evidence.claim: peso devaluation means USD holders live exceptionally well
evidence.quote: The peso's ongoing devaluation means that anyone paid in dollars, euros, or pounds can live exceptionally well in Buenos Aires for a fraction of what the same lifestyle would cost elsewhere in South America
Utilities generally cost 50 to 100 USD monthly
